commit.message:
fix: make GHCR packages public after push so Railway can pull images

After each image build+push, call GitHub REST API to set container
package visibility to public. Railway cannot authenticate to private
GHCR packages and silently rolls back to the old container when the
image pull fails with 401.

Using GITHUB_TOKEN (packages: write permission already declared in
workflow) to PATCH /orgs/{owner}/packages/container/{pkg} with
visibility=public. The step is non-fatal — warns on failure but does
not block the deploy trigger.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add weekly exercise group planner with cosine-centroid classification

Backend:
- New exercise_groups.py module with cosine-centroid classifier
  - 6 groups (Push, Pull, Legs, Shoulders, Arms, Core) defined as
    region-weight centroids
  - Exercises classified by cosine similarity to nearest centroid
  - Hardcoded weekly schedule: Tue Push+Core, Wed Legs, Thu Pull+Arms,
    Sat Shoulders+Push+Core, Sun Legs+Arms, Mon+Fri rest
  - Uses canonicalize_region() from tissue_regions.py for region
    normalization
  - Batched freshness queries (days_since_trained, RPE counts)
- New GET /api/planner/weekly-menu endpoint
- 22 tests covering cosine similarity, classification, region profiles,
  weekly menu structure, and endpoint

Frontend:
- StrengthPlannerCard now shows M T W Th F Sa Su day selector
- Each day shows exercises organized by training group with color-coded
  headers
- Exercises sorted by recency (recently trained first)
- Cross-day selection persistence: checking exercises from one day keeps
  them checked when switching to another day
- Rest days (Mon, Fri) show a rest day message
- Cross-day selection count badge

Extract GraphQL validation, add second-pass retry, refactor to eliminate duplication (#71)

* Extract GraphQL response validation into testable module

Extract the inline response parsing/validation logic from
Update-Maintainers.ps1 into GraphQLHelper.psm1 with a
Test-GraphQLResponse function. Add 14 Pester tests covering:
- Valid responses (with and without results)
- GraphQL body errors (single, multiple, partial data)
- Missing/malformed structure (no data, no search, empty, truncated)
- StrictMode safety regression tests (the exact bug that broke main)

All tests run under Set-StrictMode -Version Latest to catch
property-access bugs like the PSObject.Properties issue.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add second-pass retry for repos that fail all attempts

When a repo exhausts all 5 retry attempts (e.g. persistent HTTP 502),
queue it for a second pass after all other repos are processed. The
natural delay from processing remaining repos plus a 60s cooldown gives
GitHub's API time to recover from transient outages.

Motivated by dotnet/dotnet-api-docs failing all 5 attempts with HTTP 502
while dotnet/maui, dotnet/roslyn, and dotnet/sdk recovered on retry 2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix GraphQLHelper.psm1 review comments: update help text and fix PSObject guard

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/danmoseley/pr-dashboard/sessions/8add3188-cb08-484e-993d-98576eb3145c

* Refactor: extract Invoke-RepoMaintainerScan to eliminate duplication

Extract the per-repo scan logic (query building, pagination, retry/backoff,
response validation, node processing, activity collection) into a reusable
Invoke-RepoMaintainerScan function. Both the first pass and second-pass
retry now call this function, eliminating ~100 lines of duplicated code.

* Fix dedup bug: use Sort-Object -Unique instead of Select-Object -Unique

Select-Object -Unique only removes adjacent duplicates, so non-adjacent
duplicate logins could survive into maintainers.json. Sort-Object -Unique
does a proper global dedup.

* fix(providers): auto-populate SparseEmbeddingProviderSettings.client_options

CI on feat/dependency-management-refactor failed ~27 integration/real
tests with:
    TypeError: SparseTextEmbedding.__init__() missing 1 required
               positional argument: 'model_name'

Surfaced by the service_cards fix (5b0b2845) which correctly made the
fastembed embedder cards call the factory first then instantiate the
resolved class with whatever kwargs the dispatch chain passes in. The
dispatch chain is:

    providers/dependencies/providers.py:285
        client_options = (
            settings.client_options.as_settings()
            if settings.client_options else {}
        )
        client = await card.create_instance_async(
            target="client", **client_options
        )

When `settings.client_options` is None, kwargs is empty, and
`SparseTextEmbedding()` crashes because `model_name` is required.

Root cause is in `SparseEmbeddingProviderSettings.__init__`, which only
**updates** an existing `client_options` with model_name — it never
**creates** one when the caller didn't supply it. The sibling
`EmbeddingProviderSettings._initialize` (categories/embedding.py:178-202)
already has correct create-when-missing logic for FASTEMBED /
SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS / HUGGINGFACE_INFERENCE, but that logic was never
ported to the sparse-embedding path, so every profile that builds sparse
settings via `model_name=...` (without also passing
`client_options=FastEmbedClientOptions(model_name=...)`) ends up with
`client_options=None`. The dense-embedding path has been working because
its auto-populate logic fires first.

Fix mirrors the dense-embedding auto-populate:

  1. Resolve `provider` from raw kwargs data before pydantic validation
     runs (can't read `self.provider` at that point).
  2. If `client_options` is missing AND `model_name` is present,
     construct a provider-appropriate client options instance
     (`FastEmbedClientOptions` or `SentenceTransformersClientOptions`)
     seeded with model_name.
  3. If client_options is supplied as a dict or a ClientOptions instance,
     only inject model_name when it's genuinely missing (`setdefault` /
     `is None` guard) — don't clobber user-supplied values the way the
     previous code did.
  4. Propagate model_name into `sparse_embedding_config` as before, but
     guard against `None` to avoid `AttributeError` on the set path.

Verified locally: constructing
`FastEmbedSparseEmbeddingProviderSettings(provider=FASTEMBED,
model_name="qdrant/bm25", sparse_embedding_config=...)` now yields a
fully-populated `client_options` with model_name, and
`client_options.as_settings()` returns a dict containing
`'model_name': 'qdrant/bm25'` (pre-fix: `client_options=None`,
`as_settings()` inaccessible). This is the exact path the failing
`_testing_profile` builder took to construct CI's sparse embedding
settings.

Does not touch the dense-embedding path (already correct) or the rerank
path (has a different code structure; if it has a similar bug it will
surface as a follow-up once this unblocks the sparse failure cascade).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(service_cards): filter empty list kwargs in factory-cls handler

Follow-up to 9548b2f1 (sparse client_options auto-populate). With that
fix in place, the dispatch chain now correctly sends a fully-populated
kwargs dict to `_start_factory_cls_in_thread` — which exposed a second
bug:

    IndexError: list index out of range

Raised from deep inside fastembed's ONNX runtime setup when the
constructor receives `device_ids=[]`. Fastembed's TextEmbedding,
SparseTextEmbedding, and TextCrossEncoder all do `device_ids[0]`
unconditionally when the key is present in their kwargs — they don't
treat an empty list as "no device specified", they treat it as a
programming error that blows up at index access.

`FastEmbedClientOptions._resolve_device_settings` emits `device_ids=[]`
whenever CUDA is unavailable (CI runners, CPU-only dev envs). Before the
5b0b2845 service_cards fix, this never reached fastembed because the
handler was calling the factory itself with kwargs, and the factory
errored on `get_sparse_embedder(device_ids=[])` ("unexpected keyword
argument") before ever reaching the real constructor. The service_cards
fix correctly resolved the factory first and then instantiated with
kwargs — but that made the `device_ids=[]` quirk reachable.

The sibling `_start_cross_encoder_in_thread` handler (for the reranker
card) already filters empty lists for exactly this reason. The commit
message on 5b0b2845 claimed the quirk was reranker-specific and left
the filter out of `_start_factory_cls_in_thread`. That claim was wrong:
fastembed has this quirk across all three component types. Restoring
the filter in `_start_factory_cls_in_thread` makes both handlers
consistent and fixes the ~27 integration failures with
`IndexError: list index out of range`.

The filter is narrow — only drops values where
`isinstance(v, list) and len(v) == 0`. It preserves `cuda=False`
(bool), `threads=0` (int), `cache_dir=None`, and every non-list value
uncoditionally. Verified with a focused probe using a fake factory
and the exact kwargs shape the dispatch chain produces on CPU-only
envs.

Does not alter `_start_cross_encoder_in_thread` (already correct).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(fastembed): use `documents=` kwarg in dense embed call

Another cascade from the service_cards factory fix. With sparse
client_options auto-populating and empty `device_ids=[]` filtered out
of kwargs, CI finally reached the code path that actually CALLS
`self.client.embed(...)` in `FastEmbedEmbeddingProvider._embed_documents`
— and hit a pre-existing latent bug:

    File "src/codeweaver/providers/embedding/providers/fastembed.py",
         line 163, in <lambda>
        lambda: list(self.client.embed(
            texts=cast(Iterable[str], ready_documents), **kwargs))
    TypeError: TextEmbedding.embed() missing 1 required positional
               argument: 'documents'

fastembed's `TextEmbedding.embed()` signature is:

    embed(self, documents: Union[str, Iterable[str]], batch_size=256,
          parallel=None, **kwargs)

The first positional argument is `documents`, not `texts`. Passing the
value as `texts=...` meant `documents` never got bound and fastembed
raised the above TypeError. Pre-existed the dependency refactor; was
invisible in CI because every earlier bug in the chain blew up before
reaching this line. Now that every upstream fault is fixed (factory-cls
handler, sparse client_options, empty-list kwargs filtering), the dense
embed path actually runs end-to-end and this latent bug surfaces.

The sibling `FastEmbedSparseProvider._embed_documents` already uses
`documents=...` correctly — the dense provider copy just had a typo.

All ~20 downstream integration errors reporting
    `ProviderError: No embedding found for intent 'primary' in chunk ...`
were cascades from this: the TypeError was caught by the retry wrapper
in `base.py`, marked non-retryable, and the chunks passed on without
embeddings. The vector store upsert path in `qdrant_base._prepare_vectors`
then correctly detected missing embeddings and raised ProviderError.

Also adds a narrow ty override for `fastembed.py` suppressing two
pre-existing diagnostics that ty surfaces whenever the file is touched:

  - invalid-return-type (sparse _embed_documents/_embed_query): ty can't
    narrow `loop.run_in_executor(None, lambda: _process_output(...))`
    through the executor/lambda hop into the declared return type.
  - invalid-assignment (FastEmbedSparseProvider.client default): the
    default value is read from a TYPE_CHECKING-guarded lateimport that
    resolves to `Unknown | None` statically; real value comes via
    pydantic default_factory at runtime.

Both are pre-existing false positives unrelated to the dense embed
fix. Suppression is scoped to one file and two rules; everything
else stays strict.

Verified via `inspect.signature` that both `TextEmbedding.embed` and
`SparseTextEmbedding.embed` take `documents` as the first positional
parameter in the installed fastembed version.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: stabilize embedding-model cache and add SIGALRM timeout for real tests

Three related fixes bundled because they all target the same class of
CI failure — integration tests hanging for 20+ minutes on cold fastembed
model downloads or stuck ONNX inference:

1. Stable embedding-models cache key.

   The previous key hashed `pyproject.toml`, `uv.lock`, AND
   `codeweaver.test.toml`:

       key: embedding-models-v1-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles(
              'pyproject.toml', 'uv.lock', 'codeweaver.test.toml') }}

   Every commit on feat/dependency-management-refactor has touched
   pyproject.toml or uv.lock (they're the whole point of the branch),
   so the hash changed on every push and the cache never hit. Result:
   every integration test run cold-downloaded 100-300MB of fastembed
   + sentence-transformers weights from HuggingFace over CI egress.
   On the last ~20-minute hang, the test-under-suspicion was almost
   certainly still inside a cold model download.

   New key drops pyproject/lock and keeps only codeweaver.test.toml
   (the file that actually determines which test models are used).
   Bumps version to v2 to force one clean rebuild past any
   partially-populated v1 state, keeps v1 in restore-keys as a
   fallback for transitional commits.

   Also adds `~/.cache/fastembed/` to the cached paths — some
   fastembed versions cache outside the HuggingFace hub dir depending
   on how the model is resolved, and the extra path is cheap
   insurance.

2. Pre-download fastembed models alongside the existing ST pre-warm.

   The `Download models for real provider tests` step already
   downloads Potion 8M and MS MARCO via sentence-transformers.
   Extends it to also materialize the three fastembed models the
   testing profile uses: BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 (dense),
   prithivida/Splade_PP_en_v1 (sparse), and Qdrant/bm25 (sparse).
   Running this BEFORE the pytest step guarantees the actual test
   functions never hit a cold-download code path inside their own
   per-test timeout. With the cache fix above, the first PR run
   still cold-downloads once but subsequent runs hit the cache.

3. Signal-based timeout for real-provider tests.

   pyproject.toml sets `timeout_method = "thread"` globally, which
   can't interrupt blocking C code. Real-provider tests do CPU
   inference through ONNX runtime, which is pure C and immune to
   Python thread signals — so a genuinely stuck inference runs until
   the 6-hour GHA job limit rather than the per-test timeout kicking
   in. This is exactly the failure mode we just hit (20+ min on
   test_pipeline_handles_large_codebase).

   Fix: pass `--timeout-method=signal` alongside the existing
   `--timeout=900` override, but ONLY for real-provider tests
   (marker-based conditional). SIGALRM CAN interrupt C code, so a
   truly stuck test actually gets killed and reported as a timeout
   failure rather than a phantom hang. Unit tests keep the default
   thread method because signal has asyncio interaction caveats that
   we don't want to inherit in the fast unit suite.

Not touching: the broader CI timeout hygiene story, the fastembed
`~/.cache/fastembed/` discovery (see above for the hedge), or the
`bootstrap_settings never awaited` issue filed as knitli/codeweaver#307.

commit.message:
chore: update cockpit-inferno submodule (PTP state/grandmaster fix)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ptp): correct state detection and grandmaster parsing for statime TRACE logs

State: old code checked for 'locked'/'synchroniz'/'acquir' keywords which
don't appear in TRACE-level logs. New code parses the DEBUG
'Recommended state port N: Some(S1(...))' line — S1/M1/M2/M3 = locked,
None = acquiring.

Grandmaster: old regex expected 'grandmaster: XX:XX:XX' format. Actual
format is 'clock_uuid: [16, 231, 198, 17, 15, 4]' (decimal bytes in
GrandmasterPropertiesV1). New code parses bytes and formats as hex MAC.

Journal window: -n 80 → --since=-60s piped through grep for only the
relevant lines (Estimated offset + Recommended state port), keeping
output small while covering a full minute.

Stability fallback: if no Recommended state line found, infer locked
from offset stability (all last 5 offsets < 1ms).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
